title: Arduino IDE Setup & Features
subtitle: code, upload, create!
overview: This session introduces students to the Arduino IDE software, its installation process, interface, important features, and how to upload simple programs to Arduino UNO using beginner-friendly examples.

activity.title: Blink LED using Arduino IDE
steps: Install Arduino IDE on the computer
Connect Arduino UNO using USB cable
Click Verify button to check code
Observe LED blinking on Arduino board
Challenge: Change blinking speed from 1 second to 2 seconds

exampleCode.title: Blink LED Program

features.name: Verify Button
features.description: Checks code for errors before uploading.

features.name: Upload Button
features.description: Uploads code from computer to Arduino board.

features.name: Serial Monitor
features.description: Displays messages and sensor data from Arduino.

features.name: Library Manager
features.description: Used to install extra libraries for sensors and modules.

quiz.q: What is Arduino IDE mainly used for?
quiz.explanation: Arduino IDE is software used to write, verify, and upload programs to Arduino boards.

quiz.q: Which cable is commonly used to connect Arduino UNO to a computer?
quiz.explanation: A USB cable is used for programming and powering the Arduino UNO.

quiz.q: What does the Verify button do?
quiz.explanation: Verify button checks the code for mistakes before uploading.

quiz.q: What does the Upload button do?
quiz.explanation: Upload sends the program from computer to Arduino board.

quiz.q: Which menu contains ready-made Arduino programs?
quiz.explanation: The Examples menu contains many beginner-friendly sample projects.

quiz.q: What is the use of Serial Monitor?
quiz.explanation: Serial Monitor helps display text and sensor readings from Arduino.

quiz.q: Which board is commonly used by beginners?
quiz.explanation: Arduino UNO is the most beginner-friendly and commonly used board.

quiz.q: What happens in the Blink example?
quiz.explanation: The Blink program makes the onboard LED blink repeatedly.

quiz.q: What is delay(1000) in Arduino code?
Which pin is used in the Blink example for onboard LED?
quiz.explanation: Most Arduino UNO boards use pin 13 for the built-in LED.

quiz.q: Why do we select COM Port in Arduino IDE?
quiz.explanation: The COM Port tells the computer where the Arduino board is connected.

quiz.q: What is the role of setup() function?
options: Runs only once at the beginning
quiz.explanation: setup() runs once when Arduino starts.

quiz.q: What is the role of loop() function?
Which feature helps install sensor libraries?
quiz.explanation: Library Manager is used to install libraries for sensors and modules.

quiz.q: What should students do after changing code?
quiz.explanation: After changing code, students should verify and upload the updated program again.
